PDS Element: Core Functionality: Effective Particle and Debris Removal
Justification: The primary function is to effectively remove particulate matter and debris, maintaining performance over the product's lifespan, with a focus on material separation for recycling.; Filtration: Utilize HEPA filtration (99.97% of particles down to 0.3 microns), with specific attention to efficiency for PM2.5 particles.; Performance maintenance: Ensure filtration and suction performance are maintained throughout the product's expected lifespan, with clear, user-friendly indicators for filter replacement.; Material separation: Design the dustbin for easy hygienic emptying and recyclability. Facilitate easy separation of the dustbin from the vacuum body for end-of-life recycling.; Suction power: Target >150 Air Watts (AW) measured under standardized conditions (e.g., IEC 60312-1) on the highest power setting.; Airflow rate: Target >30 L/s measured at 20 hPa with standardized accessories.

Minimize environmental impact through reduced energy consumption during operation, charging, and throughout the lifecycle.; Motor efficiency: Target motor efficiency of >90% at optimal load.; Standby power: Standby power consumption of the unit and charger not to exceed 0.5W.; Operational energy consumption: Achieve Energy Star certification requirements. Target <40 kWh/year based on standardized usage.; Intelligent power management: Implement intelligent power management for optimized energy use across different modes, considering the energy consumed by the charging process.; Manufacturing & Transportation energy: Develop concrete targets and steps for reducing energy consumed during manufacturing and transportation, potentially encouraging renewable energy use.

Prioritize materials with minimal environmental impact, enabling a circular economy through recyclability, responsible sourcing, and ease of separation.; Recycled content: Mandate a minimum of 50% post-consumer recycled (PCR) content for all plastic and metal components, targeting 75% for structural parts. Prioritize high-quality recycled streams.; Material types: Prioritize mono-materials and easily separable combinations. Minimize the number of different material types used. Avoid composite materials and glues; if essential, provide LCA justification and a disassembly/recycling plan.; Recyclability labeling: All plastic components must be clearly labeled with resin identification codes (e.g., PP, ABS) embossed in the mold. Ensure durability of labels.; Design for disassembly: Facilitate easy disassembly for repair and recycling, aiming for tool-less access for user-serviceable parts.; Biodegradable materials: Use cautiously for specific, controlled applications (e.g., certified compostable packaging), avoiding where durability or uncertain end-of-life processing is a concern.

Extend product lifespan to reduce waste and the need for frequent replacements, supported by rigorous testing, modular design, and maintainability.; Product lifespan: Target 10 years of typical use or 2000 operational hours, validated through comprehensive and representative accelerated life testing. Define 'typical use' rigorously.; Motor durability: Main motor rated for >5000 operational cycles, validated through realistic wear-and-tear testing.; Wear-resistant components: Key components (e.g., brush roll, seals) designed for enhanced wear resistance and longevity.; Modular design: Design for easy replacement of key wear parts (filters, brush rolls, battery if applicable) with minimal effort and without specialized tools. Balance modularity with overall robustness.; Spare parts availability: Ensure availability of spare parts for at least 7 years post-production, with clear documentation on their cost and ease of replacement.; Testing protocols: Reference relevant ISO, ASTM, or IEC standards for structural integrity, motor durability, and component wear testing.

PDS Element: User Experience: Ease of Use and Ergonomics
Justification: Ensure comfortable, intuitive, and efficient operation, including simplified maintenance, repair, and accessibility.; Weight: Target total product weight under 5kg, with ergonomic design for comfortable handling, considering weight distribution and balance.; Maintenance tasks: Simplify dustbin emptying and filter cleaning for hygiene, minimal dust re-emission, and ease of disassembly using simple tools.; Control interface: Design intuitive controls with minimal buttons (e.g., <3 primary controls) providing responsive feedback.; Disassembly for repair: Ensure ease of disassembly using simple tools (e.g., Phillips head screwdriver) without compromising structural integrity or sealing.; Handling: Ergonomic design for comfortable grip and maneuverability, considering reach for comfortable cleaning.

Minimize operational noise to enhance user comfort and reduce disturbance through integrated acoustic design.; Maximum noise level: Target a maximum of 70 dB(A) measured at 1 meter on a hard floor.; Noise reduction methods: Achieve target through optimized airflow, motor enclosure, and judicious use of lightweight, acoustically effective dampening materials considered during initial design.; Material impact: Avoid excessive material additions for noise reduction that compromise weight, recyclability, or introduce toxicity.

Design for modularity, easy component replacement, clear repair guidance, and tool-less maintenance.; Modularity: Key components (motor, battery, electronics, brush head) accessible and replaceable with simple tools.; Tool-less maintenance: Specify tool-less disassembly for user-level maintenance (e.g., filter changes, brush roll access).; Repair guides: Provide clear, illustrated repair guides and potentially video tutorials for common issues.; Spare parts: Ensure spare parts are available for at least 7 years, with a target for their cost relative to the original product cost.

Ensure superior indoor air quality by effectively capturing fine particles and allergens throughout the filter's lifespan.; Filtration standard: Utilize HEPA filtration (99.97% of particles down to 0.3 microns).; Filter materials: Prioritize filters made from mono-materials that can be easily separated and recycled, or reusable filter systems. If disposable, ensure easy separation from housings.; Filter lifespan: Define filter lifespan and ease of replacement, ensuring performance is maintained and replacement is cost-effective.; Cost-effectiveness: Verify the cost-effectiveness of HEPA filtration at the target price point and explore simpler replacement indicators.

Provide adequate capacity for typical cleaning sessions and ensure hygienic, efficient emptying with minimal dust re-emission.; Capacity: For bagless models, target 1.5-2L capacity sized for typical household use.; Emptying: Design for easy, hygienic emptying using a sealed mechanism with minimal dust re-emission. Ensure easy separation of the dustbin from the vacuum body.; Material: Dustbins made from durable, easily cleanable, and recyclable plastic.; Bagged option: If bags are used, they must be made from easily compostable or recyclable materials, with a mechanism for sealing and removal that prevents leakage.

Guarantee user safety through standard features and design considerations that do not impede repair or recycling.; Standard features: Incorporate automatic overheat protection and secure electrical connections.; Material safety: Ensure electrical components and casings are fire retardant and non-toxic.; Repair/Recycling compatibility: Safety features must not require destructive disassembly or specialized tools.; Battery safety (if applicable): Battery must be easily removable and designed for safe handling and recycling, with safety features not preventing removal.

Minimize ongoing costs through energy efficiency, durability, affordable recyclable components, and clear warranty.; Operating costs: Prioritize energy efficiency and durability to reduce long-term expenses.; Replacement parts: Ensure replacement parts (filters, brush rolls, batteries) are readily available, competitively priced, and designed for easy replacement.; Warranty: Clearly define the warranty period as part of the total cost of ownership.

Adopt a modern, clean, and minimalist design that communicates durability and quality using sustainable materials.; Design style: Modern, clean, and minimalist with natural colors achieved through material choice and processing, not coatings that hinder recyclability.; Material perception: Materials should convey durability and quality, balancing sustainable properties with aesthetic appeal.; Form: Avoid complex shapes that increase manufacturing difficulty or hinder disassembly and recycling.

Design for disassembly, clear material labeling, and implement a take-back program for maximum material recovery.; Disassembly: Design for disassembly using simple, standardized fasteners (e.g., Torx screws) accessible with common household tools, and avoiding glues where possible.; Material labeling: All components clearly labeled with material type for recycling.; Take-back program: Implement a product take-back program with defined logistics (collection points, mail-in, retailer partnerships) to ensure responsible end-of-life management.; Material recovery: Aim for maximum material recovery and recycling, with clear pathways for each component, facilitated by easy disassembly.

Minimize environmental impact through material choice, design, responsible sourcing, and consideration of transportation footprint.; Recycled content: Minimum 80% post-consumer recycled (PCR) content for all packaging materials.; Recyclability/Compostability: All packaging components must be recyclable or compostable.; Right-sizing: Design for 'right-sizing' to minimize void space and transportation emissions, balancing with product protection.; Material restrictions: Avoid single-use plastics and styrofoam.; Reusable packaging: Consider reusable packaging for spare parts and returned products, integrating into logistics.

Provide a versatile cleaning solution with durable, easy-to-use, and recyclable attachments.; Attachments: Design attachments for durability, ease of cleaning, and recyclability.; Attachment system: Robust, easy to use, and cost-effective connection mechanism.; Disassembly: Ensure attachments can be easily disassembled for repair or recycling.; Floor head: Prioritize a single, well-designed floor head with simple adjustments for versatility across common floor types, ensuring durability and ease of cleaning.
